creator: kbowling@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to e94pasch from comment #8)
Thanks, can you do 'top -PSH' instead?  I will explain my reasoning so you can follow along.  A stream is ordered and transcends the network stack on one core.  Therefore it is dependent on the single core performance.  Adding more work into the network stack, like shaping, can therefore result in the bw limits you are seeing.  So, after looking at the revised top info (PSH will show cores, system, and threads) we can confirm if this is the case.
